name: Bhai Sarabjit Singh Rangila
body: Bhai Sarabjit Singh Rangila is regarded as one of the finest Kirtaniyas today. His melodious voice combined with rigorous practice over the years can be appreciated in his performance of Gurmat Sangeet and Shastri Sangeet. Bhai Sarabjit Singh Rangila was born on 13th August 1952 in the city of Bareilly, Uttar Pradesh. He was born into the home of renowned ‘Puratan Kirtaniya’, Bhai Sukha Singh Ji Almast who was originally from the city of Quetta (Koita) in Balochistan which is now in Pakistan. Bhai Sarabjit Singh Rangila began his journey into music at the tender age of 5. His first teacher was his father, Bhai Sukha Singh Ji. Subsequently, he went on to learn from Prof. Partap Singh Ji of Kashipur and then from Ustad Nasir Ahmed Khan, a vocalist belonging to the Delhi Gharana of music. It is notable to mention that Rangila Ji continues to learn and hone his skills even today and his current teacher is Ustad Prof. Ranjit Singh Ji from the United States.

Besides his training in Kirtan, Rangila Ji has also passed 10+2 from the Udayraj Hindu Inter College located in Kashipur, Uttrakhand. His personal interests include listening to and singing in various Raags, reading books on spiritualism and also traveling around the world. He has traveled to many countries including Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, China, the United Kingdom, Canada, the United States of America and more. Currently, he is based mostly in New York and teaches Kirtan to many children and adults as well.
Bhai Sarabjit Singh Rangila has received numerous awards throughout his career and some of them are as listed below:

-Awarded the Bhai Mardana Award (1983-1988) -Awarded by Gurudwara Gyan Prakash , Jawaddi Kalan , Ludhiana ( 1992-1996) -Awarded for Gurmat Sangeet , Frankfurt , Germany (1997) -Awarded for Classical Vocalist , New York , USA (1997) -Awarded by Darbar E Khalsa as Bhai Mardana International Award , California (2002) -Awarded the Bhai Mardana Award , Ferozpur -Awarded by Naam Ras Kirtan Darbar, Singapore -Awarded by Takhat Keshgarh Sahib, Anandpur Sahib -Awarded by Gurmat Sangeet Vibhag , Punjabi University Patiala (2002-2003)
The information above was obtained from Bhai Sarabjit Singh Rangila and if anyone has more recordings of Rangila Ji to share, please contact us.
